channel.title: Artometrics Reports
channel.description: Data reports on culture, power, and the creative economy

item.title: How Oscars, Grammys, and Nobels Convert Attention Into Prestige
item.description: A cross-medium look at how prizes turn visibility into legitimacy across film, music, and science.

item.title: California and Texas: Where Rival State Systems Diverge
item.description: What the two largest U.S. states share—and where their economies, exports, and risks part ways.

item.title: How the Celtics Turned 1960s Dominance Into Permanent Expectation
item.description: Boston’s championship inheritance still shapes every modern season.

item.title: Cities as Operating Systems of Exports, Culture, and Scarcity
item.description: A geo-economics frame treats cities as layered systems of trade, culture, and infrastructure.

item.title: The Cowboys’ Gap Between Brand Value and Championship Conversion
item.description: Dallas remains the NFL’s clearest split between global brand power and an aging postseason engine.

item.title: When Films, Music, and Games Become National Exports
item.description: A cultural-economics frame treats soft-power industries as export goods.

item.title: How the Dodgers Built Baseball’s Modern Consistency Machine
item.description: Los Angeles turned payroll, development, and depth into recurring October access.

item.title: Why the U.S., China, and Germany Dominate Export Debate
item.description: A three-country comparison of platforms, factories, and machinery.

item.title: How Movies Become Famous on IMDb’s Public Scoreboard
item.description: Title fields, ratings, and box-office references explain how films accumulate attention.

item.title: How the Lakers Convert Stars Into Championship Eras
item.description: Los Angeles basketball’s glamour machine turns market gravity into title windows.

item.title: How League Money and Roster Rules Shape Star Systems
item.description: Cross-sport rules, payroll, and scarcity show why teams become culturally meaningful.

item.title: How Catalog Depth and Reinvention Make Pop Stars Famous
item.description: MusicBrainz-style metadata links fame to release depth, genre travel, and reinvention.

item.title: What Nations Sell—and What That Says About Identity
item.description: A country-level atlas treats exports as identity: what states sell and need.

item.title: New York and San Francisco: Command City vs. Invention City
item.description: Why the two are paired in debate—and where their economic and cultural systems diverge.

item.title: Who Gets Remembered in the Global Pantheon of Fame?
item.description: Biography-scale fame data ask where collective memory clusters.

item.title: The Patriots’ Brady–Belichick Era as a System Shock
item.description: Two decades of New England football compressed a century of legacy into one dynasty.

item.title: Mapping the Public-Domain Canon Through Project Gutenberg
item.description: Gutenberg metadata charts language gravity, era effects, and author memory.

item.title: How American Cities Build Distinct Sports Identities
item.description: Markets, leagues, and championship droughts explain why fan cultures differ by city.

item.title: San Francisco as an Invention Machine Under Housing Pressure
item.description: A city-identity report through software exports, scarcity, and neighborhoods.

item.title: Best, Worst, and Most Misleading Sports Dynasties
item.description: A cross-league index of championship mass, conversion, market value, and fan pain.

item.title: The Yankees as Baseball’s Benchmark for Expectation and Scale
item.description: More than titles: New York sets the standard for droughts, payroll, and magnitude.

item.title: Which Airlines Have the Worst Safety Records?
item.description: Carrier-level accident and fatality data ask which airlines stand out for poor safety outcomes.

item.title: Who Drinks the Most Alcohol Per Capita?
item.description: Country-level consumption data map which nations drink the most per person—and how that has shifted.

item.title: What Does a Slice of Pizza Really Cost?
item.description: Price and calorie data across pizza menus show what diners pay in money and energy.

item.title: Beyoncé and Taylor Swift, Line by Line
item.description: A lyric-level comparison of two era-defining catalogs—theme, language, and structure.

item.title: What Twenty Years of Big Mac Prices Say About Purchasing Power
item.description: Global burger prices illustrate how currency and wages shape the real cost of everyday goods.

item.title: How Far Do Portland’s Biketown Rides Actually Go?
item.description: Trip-level bike-share data measures typical distance and duration across Biketown journeys.

item.title: How Long Number-One Hits Stay on Top of the Hot 100
item.description: Since 1958, most chart-toppers lasted weeks; a few held for months—and streaming rewrote the pattern.

item.title: How the Top of the Billboard Chart Got More Crowded
item.description: Large-scale Hot 100 data show how rankings compress at the summit.

item.title: Which Board Games Combine Crowd Scale With Elite Ratings?
item.description: Ratings and popularity across thousands of titles find games that win both mass attention and esteem.

item.title: Which Broadway Musicals Ran Longest and Grossed Most?
item.description: Performance and box-office records map the shows that dominated by longevity and revenue.

item.title: How Long Do CEOs Last Before They Leave?
item.description: Thousands of executive-exit records measure tenure length and how departures cluster.

item.title: How Whale and Dolphin Sizes Cluster by Family
item.description: Morphometric data map which cetacean lineages produce the giants.

item.title: Which Christmas Novels Anchor the Holiday Shelf?
item.description: A Gutenberg holiday corpus shows which seasonal novels still define the Christmas canon.

item.title: Which Holiday Songs Never Leave the Charts?
item.description: Decades of Christmas-song chart data identify the standards that return every season.

item.title: How Population Scale Relates to Prosperity Across Countries
item.description: Factbook indicators compare nations on size and wealth—where scale and prosperity align or diverge.

item.title: Which College Majors Pay Most—and Which Carry Job Risk?
item.description: Earnings and unemployment by major show where degrees pay off and where risk is higher.

item.title: Who Ranks Highest on Comic-Book Power Scales?
item.description: Character attributes across Marvel and DC ask who sits atop power rankings.

item.title: How Bitter and Strong Is American Craft Beer?
item.description: Brewery-level IBU and ABV data map bitterness and strength across U.S. craft producers.

item.title: Which Shows Dominated Emmy Season—and How Lopsided Was It?
item.description: Nomination and win records reveal which series swept hardware—and how concentrated awards became.

item.title: Which U.S. States Exercise the Most?
item.description: State-level activity rates compare where Americans work out most.

item.title: Which Fast-Food Chains Pack the Most Calories Per Item?
item.description: Menu nutrition data identify the highest-calorie items across major chains.

item.title: How Human Life Expectancy Doubled in Five Centuries
item.description: Survival data from the 1500s to 2015 map who gained years of life—and who lagged.

item.title: Who Generates the Most Plastic Waste—and Who Mismanages It?
item.description: Country-level plastic generation and mismanagement rates show where leakage concentrates.

item.title: Which Horror Movies Returned Multiples—and Which Burned Cash?
item.description: Budget and box-office data for thousands of horror titles separate winners from costly misses.

item.title: Did Horror Get Better as the Catalog Exploded?
item.description: Ratings across tens of thousands of horror films ask whether quality rose with volume.

item.title: Which Storms Hit Puerto Rico Hardest?
item.description: Hurricane records compare wind, deaths, and frequency for the island’s most damaging storms.

item.title: Where Wastewater Plants Serve the Most People
item.description: Facility-level data map which plants serve the largest populations.

item.title: How U.S. Incarceration Rates Diverged by State
item.description: Long-run imprisonment data track national trends and which states pulled away.

item.title: How Concentrated Is the World’s Linguistic Diversity?
item.description: Glottolog family data measure how uneven language diversity is at the family level.

item.title: How LEGO Set Complexity Evolved Across Themes
item.description: Official set metadata traces rising piece counts and theme differences.

item.title: Did Longer Medium Posts Earn More Applause?
item.description: Metadata from tens of thousands of Medium articles tests whether length predicts claps.

item.title: Which National Parks Draw the Crowds?
item.description: Visitation time series identify America’s busiest parks and how attendance has trended.

item.title: Which Netflix Titles Consumed the Most Viewing Hours?
item.description: Engagement metrics Netflix reports show which titles absorbed the most hours.

item.title: How Netflix’s Catalog Mix Shifted Between Films and Series
item.description: Title-level library data track the balance of movies and TV over time.

item.title: How Nuclear Test Yields Shifted Through the Atomic Age
item.description: Historical test records map yield, purpose, and timing across decades.

item.title: Which NYC Cuisines Score Highest on Health Inspections?
item.description: Restaurant grades across New York show how scores distribute by cuisine.

item.title: Where Pixar’s Critic Streak Broke: Cars and Lightyear
item.description: Data on Pixar’s canon track scores and the titles that interrupted a long critical run.

item.title: Which Subjects Dominate the Project Gutenberg Shelf?
item.description: Public-domain metadata show which topics crowd the free-library canon.

item.title: How U.S. Radio Formats Map Across the Dial
item.description: Station-level format data chart where music, talk, and niche programming concentrate.

item.title: Which Ramen Brands Earn the Highest Ratings?
item.description: Taster scores across thousands of instant ramen products identify top brands.

item.title: Which Albums Sit at the Peak of Rolling Stone’s Canon?
item.description: Canonical rankings identify the records Rolling Stone places at the summit.

item.title: Who Ruled Rome Longest—and Did Dynasty Predict Tenure?
item.description: Reign-length data test whether dynastic succession meant longer time on the throne.

item.title: Where U.S. Student-Body Diversity Is Highest
item.description: School-level composition data map where diversity concentrates.

item.title: How Word Count Is Distributed Across the Holmes Canon
item.description: Text-length analysis of Sherlock Holmes stories through Conan’s corpus.

item.title: Who Guest-Starred Most Often on The Simpsons?
item.description: Guest-appearance records count celebrity density across seasons.

item.title: Which Super Bowl Ads Won the Post-Game Internet?
item.description: Ad-spot data track which commercials captured attention after the whistle.

item.title: When Did Prestige TV Peak—and Did Critics Agree?
item.description: Golden-age scores compare critical and audience verdicts as prestige TV rose and cooled.

item.title: Where UK Museums Sit on the Deprivation Map
item.description: Museum locations and deprivation bands show how institutions map onto inequality.

item.title: Which UN Votes Split the Chamber Most Sharply?
item.description: Voting records identify resolutions that divided member states.

item.title: Which U.S. PhD Fields Expanded Fastest?
item.description: Doctorate production data show which disciplines grew quickest.

item.title: How Far Did Average U.S. Tuition Climb by State?
item.description: State tuition time series measure the rise in college costs.

item.title: Which States Show Up at the Ballot Box?
item.description: Election turnout data compare state participation rates.

item.title: Did Critical Acclaim Track Commercial Success in Video Games?
item.description: A large games slice tests whether critic scores correlated with commercial outcomes.

item.title: Which Steam Games Earned the Most Playtime Per Dollar?
item.description: Ownership and play data identify titles that delivered the most hours relative to price.

item.title: How Concentrated Is Wealth at the Top of the Distribution?
item.description: Wealth and income shares measure how assets pile up at the upper end.

item.title: Which Web Pages Load Fastest—and What Metrics Move Together?
item.description: Performance metrics show speed leaders and which technical measures correlate.

item.title: Does Wine Price Predict Critic Points?
item.description: A hundred thousand wine ratings test whether cost tracks scores.

item.title: How UNESCO Sites Grew Across Scandinavia, 2004–2022
item.description: UNESCO heritage counts track how Scandinavian inventories expanded.

item.title: What Pokémon’s Franchise Actually Comprises
item.description: Beyond the headline revenue figure: games, cards, merchandise, and film.

item.title: Which Hospitals Still Fail the 30-Day Readmission Standard?
item.description: CMS HRRP data show where states, conditions, and ownership types exceed benchmarks.

item.title: Julius Caesar and the Cost of Managing Power
item.description: A psychonomic reading of Caesar asks what ambition cost the Roman political household he ruled.

item.title: The Giants’ Arc From New York Gothams to Oracle Park
item.description: A franchise history from 1883 New York origins to a modern Bay Area ballclub.

item.title: How Golden State Went From Irrelevant to Dynasty
item.description: Decades of losing before a modern dynasty remade Bay Area basketball.

item.title: Coffee’s Place in Global Trade and Daily Life
item.description: Commodity and consumption data place coffee among the world’s largest traded goods.

item.title: How H3 Built a YouTube Network Beyond One Channel
item.description: Since 2011, Ethan and Hila Klein scaled channels, a podcast, and a brand.

item.title: What Empire Chronicles Leave Out: Who Got Rich
item.description: A historical-economics lens asks who captured wealth when rulers claimed order.

item.title: How the World’s Biggest Media Franchises Actually Make Money
item.description: Revenue maps for more than 100 franchises show where valuable IP earns across media.

item.title: What a Century of Anime Titles Reveals About the Industry
item.description: MyAnimeList metadata on more than 13,000 titles traces Japanese animation as a production system.
